# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration

# docker-compose.yml for testing environment
# File: docker-compose.yml
---
version: '3.8'

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
    container_name: es-snapshot-node
    environment:
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - path.repo=/usr/share/elasticsearch/backup
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
    volumes:
      - es-data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - es-backup:/usr/share/elasticsearch/backup
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - elastic

  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    container_name: minio-s3
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: minioadmin
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: minioadmin123
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
      - "9001:9001"
    volumes:
      - minio-data:/data
    networks:
      - elastic

volumes:
  es-data:
    driver: local
  es-backup:
    driver: local
  minio-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  elastic:
    driver: bridge

---
# File: config/snapshot-config.yml
# Main snapshot configuration file
snapshot:
  cluster:
    name: production-cluster
    hosts:
      - https://es-node-01:9200
      - https://es-node-02:9200
      - https://es-node-03:9200
    auth:
      username: "${ES_USERNAME:elastic}"
      password: "${ES_PASSWORD}"
      api_key: "${ES_API_KEY}"
    ssl:
      verify_certs: true
      ca_certs: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/ca.crt

  repositories:
    # S3-based repository configuration
    s3_repository:
      name: s3-backup-repo
      type: s3
      settings:
        bucket: elasticsearch-snapshots
        region: us-east-1
        base_path: backups/production
        compress: true
        server_side_encryption: true
        storage_class: standard_ia
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
        chunk_size: 1gb
        readonly: false
        canned_acl: private
        buffer_size: 100mb
      credentials:
        access_key: "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"
        secret_key: "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"
        role_arn: "${AWS_ROLE_ARN}"

    # File share repository configuration
    fs_repository:
      name: fs-backup-repo
      type: fs
      settings:
        location: /usr/share/elasticsearch/backup
        compress: true
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
        chunk_size: null
        readonly: false

    # Azure repository configuration
    azure_repository:
      name: azure-backup-repo
      type: azure
      settings:
        container: elasticsearch-snapshots
        base_path: backups/production
        compress: true
        chunk_size: 256mb
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
      credentials:
        account: "${AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT}"
        key: "${AZURE_STORAGE_KEY}"

    # GCS repository configuration
    gcs_repository:
      name: gcs-backup-repo
      type: gcs
      settings:
        bucket: elasticsearch-snapshots
        base_path: backups/production
        compress: true
        chunk_size: 256mb
        max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
        max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
      credentials:
        service_account: "${GCS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON}"

  # Default repository to use
  default_repository: s3-backup-repo

---
# File: config/snapshot-policies.yml
# Snapshot lifecycle management policies
policies:
  # Hourly snapshots for critical indices
  hourly_critical:
    name: hourly-critical-snapshot
    schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"  # Every hour at minute 0
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "transactions-*"
        - "orders-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: hourly-critical
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 48h
      min_count: 24
      max_count: 48

  # Daily full snapshots
  daily_full:
    name: daily-full-snapshot
    schedule: "0 0 2 * * ?"  # Every day at 2:00 AM
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      feature_states:
        - "geoip"
        - "security"
      metadata:
        policy: daily-full
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 30d
      min_count: 7
      max_count: 30

  # Weekly snapshots for long-term retention
  weekly_archive:
    name: weekly-archive-snapshot
    schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"  # Every Sunday at 3:00 AM
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: weekly-archive
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 365d
      min_count: 4
      max_count: 52

  # Monthly compliance snapshots
  monthly_compliance:
    name: monthly-compliance-snapshot
    schedule: "0 0 4 1 * ?"  # 1st of every month at 4:00 AM
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "audit-*"
        - "compliance-*"
        - "logs-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: monthly-compliance
        team: compliance
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 2555d  # ~7 years
      min_count: 12
      max_count: 84

---
# File: config/restore-config.yml
# Restoration configuration templates
restore:
  templates:
    # Full cluster restore
    full_restore:
      name: full-cluster-restore
      description: "Restore entire cluster from snapshot"
      indices: "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      include_aliases: true
      partial: false
      rename_pattern: null
      rename_replacement: null
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0  # Speed up restore, increase after
      ignore_index_settings:
        - "index.refresh_interval"

    # Single index restore
    single_index_restore:
      name: single-index-restore
      description: "Restore a single index from snapshot"
      ignore_unavailable: false
      include_global_state: false
      include_aliases: true
      partial: false
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0
      ignore_index_settings:
        - "index.refresh_interval"

    # Restore to different index name
    renamed_restore:
      name: renamed-index-restore
      description: "Restore index with different name for comparison"
      ignore_unavailable: false
      include_global_state: false
      include_aliases: false
      partial: false
      rename_pattern: "(.+)"
      rename_replacement: "restored-$1"
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0
        index.number_of_shards: null  # Keep original shard count

    # Point-in-time restore for disaster recovery
    disaster_recovery:
      name: disaster-recovery-restore
      description: "Full disaster recovery restoration"
      indices: "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      include_aliases: true
      partial: true
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0
        index.routing.allocation.require._name: null
      ignore_index_settings:
        - "index.refresh_interval"
        - "index.routing.allocation.include._tier_preference"

  # Post-restore actions
  post_restore:
    wait_for_completion: true
    timeout: 3600s
    verify_indices: true
    update_replicas:
      enabled: true
      target_replicas: 1
      delay_seconds: 300
    refresh_indices: true
    validate_doc_counts: true

---
# File: config/notifications.yml
# Notification configuration for snapshot events
notifications:
  enabled: true

  channels:
    slack:
      enabled: true
      webhook_url: "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}"
      channel: "#ops-elasticsearch"
      username: "ES Snapshot Bot"
      icon_emoji: ":floppy_disk:"
      notify_on:
        - snapshot_success
        - snapshot_failure
        - restore_success
        - restore_failure
        - retention_cleanup
        - repository_error

    email:
      enabled: true
      smtp:
        host: smtp.company.com
        port: 587
        use_tls: true
        username: "${SMTP_USERNAME}"
        password: "${SMTP_PASSWORD}"
      from: "elasticsearch-snapshots@company.com"
      recipients:
        - "ops-team@company.com"
        - "oncall@company.com"
      notify_on:
        - snapshot_failure
        - restore_failure
        - repository_error

    pagerduty:
      enabled: true
      service_key: "${PAGERDUTY_SERVICE_KEY}"
      notify_on:
        - snapshot_failure
        - repository_error

  templates:
    snapshot_success:
      title: "✅ Snapshot Completed Successfully"
      body: |
        Snapshot: {{ snapshot_name }}
        Repository: {{ repository }}
        Duration: {{ duration }}
        Indices: {{ index_count }}
        Total Shards: {{ total_shards }}
        Successful Shards: {{ successful_shards }}
        Size: {{ size }}

    snapshot_failure:
      title: "❌ Snapshot Failed"
      body: |
        Snapshot: {{ snapshot_name }}
        Repository: {{ repository }}
        Error: {{ error_message }}
        Failed Shards: {{ failed_shards }}
        Timestamp: {{ timestamp }}

    restore_success:
      title: "✅ Restore Completed Successfully"
      body: |
        Snapshot: {{ snapshot_name }}
        Repository: {{ repository }}
        Restored Indices: {{ restored_indices }}
        Duration: {{ duration }}

    restore_failure:
      title: "❌ Restore Failed"
      body: |
        Snapshot: {{ snapshot_name }}
        Repository: {{ repository }}
        Error: {{ error_message }}
        Timestamp: {{ timestamp }}

---
# File: scripts/manage-snapshots.sh
# #!/bin/bash
# Snapshot management script
# Usage: ./manage-snapshots.sh [command] [options]

---
# File: config/elasticsearch-keystore.yml
# Keystore settings to be added to Elasticsearch nodes
keystore:
  s3:
    - key: s3.client.default.access_key
      value: "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}"
    - key: s3.client.default.secret_key
      value: "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}"
    - key: s3.client.default.session_token
      value: "${AWS_SESSION_TOKEN}"

  azure:
    - key: azure.client.default.account
      value: "${AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT}"
    - key: azure.client.default.key
      value: "${AZURE_STORAGE_KEY}"

  gcs:
    - key: gcs.client.default.credentials_file
      value: "${GCS_CREDENTIALS_FILE}"

---
# File: api-calls/01-register-s3-repository.yml
# Register S3 snapshot repository
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo
  body:
    type: s3
    settings:
      bucket: elasticsearch-snapshots
      region: us-east-1
      base_path: backups/production
      compress: true
      server_side_encryption: true
      storage_class: standard_ia
      max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
      chunk_size: 1gb

---
# File: api-calls/02-register-fs-repository.yml
# Register filesystem snapshot repository
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_snapshot/fs-backup-repo
  body:
    type: fs
    settings:
      location: /usr/share/elasticsearch/backup
      compress: true
      max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb

---
# File: api-calls/03-verify-repository.yml
# Verify repository is accessible
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/_verify

---
# File: api-calls/04-create-snapshot.yml
# Create a manual snapshot
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/manual-snapshot-{timestamp}
  params:
    wait_for_completion: false
  body:
    indices: "*"
    ignore_unavailable: true
    include_global_state: true
    partial: false
    metadata:
      taken_by: operations-team
      reason: manual-backup
      environment: production

---
# File: api-calls/05-create-slm-policy-daily.yml
# Create SLM policy for daily snapshots
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_slm/policy/daily-full-snapshot
  body:
    schedule: "0 0 2 * * ?"
    name: "<daily-snap-{now/d}>"
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
    retention:
      expire_after: 30d
      min_count: 7
      max_count: 30

---
# File: api-calls/06-create-slm-policy-hourly.yml
# Create SLM policy for hourly critical snapshots
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_slm/policy/hourly-critical-snapshot
  body:
    schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"
    name: "<hourly-critical-{now/H}>"
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "transactions-*"
        - "orders-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
    retention:
      expire_after: 2d
      min_count: 24
      max_count: 48

---
# File: api-calls/07-create-slm-policy-weekly.yml
# Create SLM policy for weekly archive snapshots
request:
  method: PUT
  path: /_slm/policy/weekly-archive-snapshot
  body:
    schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"
    name: "<weekly-archive-{now/w}>"
    repository: s3-backup-repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
    retention:
      expire_after: 365d
      min_count: 4
      max_count: 52

---
# File: api-calls/08-restore-full-snapshot.yml
# Restore from a snapshot - full cluster
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot_name}/_restore
  params:
    wait_for_completion: false
  body:
    indices: "*"
    ignore_unavailable: true
    include_global_state: true
    include_aliases: true
    partial: false
    index_settings:
      index.number_of_replicas: 0
    ignore_index_settings:
      - "index.refresh_interval"

---
# File: api-calls/09-restore-single-index.yml
# Restore a single index from snapshot
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot_name}/_restore
  params:
    wait_for_completion: true
  body:
    indices: "{index_name}"
    ignore_unavailable: false
    include_global_state: false
    include_aliases: true
    partial: false
    index_settings:
      index.number_of_replicas: 0
    ignore_index_settings:
      - "index.refresh_interval"

---
# File: api-calls/10-restore-renamed-index.yml
# Restore index with a different name (for comparison/testing)
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot_name}/_restore
  params:
    wait_for_completion: true
  body:
    indices: "{index_name}"
    ignore_unavailable: false
    include_global_state: false
    include_aliases: false
    partial: false
    rename_pattern: "(.+)"
    rename_replacement: "restored-$1"
    index_settings:
      index.number_of_replicas: 0
    ignore_index_settings:
      - "index.refresh_interval"

---
# File: api-calls/11-get-snapshot-status.yml
# Check snapshot status
requests:
  # Get all snapshots in repository
  list_snapshots:
    method: GET
    path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/_all

  # Get specific snapshot status
  snapshot_status:
    method: GET
    path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot_name}/_status

  # Get SLM policy status
  slm_status:
    method: GET
    path: /_slm/policy

  # Get SLM stats
  slm_stats:
    method: GET
    path: /_slm/stats

  # Get repository info
  repository_info:
    method: GET
    path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo

  # Get ongoing snapshot/restore operations
  current_operations:
    method: GET
    path: /_snapshot/_status

---
# File: api-calls/12-delete-snapshot.yml
# Delete a specific snapshot
request:
  method: DELETE
  path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot_name}

---
# File: api-calls/13-execute-slm-policy.yml
# Manually execute an SLM policy
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_slm/policy/daily-full-snapshot/_execute

---
# File: api-calls/14-execute-retention.yml
# Manually execute retention cleanup
request:
  method: POST
  path: /_slm/_execute_retention

---
# File: monitoring/snapshot-monitoring.yml
# Monitoring and alerting configuration for snapshots
monitoring:
  health_checks:
    # Check snapshot repository connectivity
    repository_check:
      schedule: "*/5 * * * *"  # Every 5 minutes
      action: verify_repository
      repositories:
        - s3-backup-repo
        - fs-backup-repo
      alert_on_failure: true

    # Check SLM policy execution status
    slm_policy_check:
      schedule: "*/15 * * * *"  # Every 15 minutes
      action: check_slm_status
      policies:
        - daily-full-snapshot
        - hourly-critical-snapshot
        - weekly-archive-snapshot
      alert_conditions:
        - last_failure_within: 1h
        - consecutive_failures: 3
        - missed_schedule: true

    # Check snapshot age
    snapshot_age_check:
      schedule: "0 */6 * * *"  # Every 6 hours
      action: check_latest_snapshot_age
      max_age:
        daily: 26h  # Allow 2-hour buffer
        hourly: 2h  # Allow 1-hour buffer
        weekly: 8d  # Allow 1-day buffer
      alert_on_stale: true

    # Check snapshot size trends
    snapshot_size_check:
      schedule: "0 8 * * *"  # Daily at 8 AM
      action: check_snapshot_size_trend
      alert_conditions:
        - size_increase_percent: 50  # Alert if 50% larger than avg
        - size_decrease_percent: 30  # Alert if 30% smaller than avg
      comparison_window: 7d

  # Elasticsearch Watcher alerts
  watchers:
    snapshot_failure_alert:
      trigger:
        schedule:
          interval: 5m
      input:
        http:
          request:
            host: localhost
            port: 9200
            path: /_slm/stats
            scheme: https
      condition:
        compare:
          ctx.payload.stats.snapshots_failed:
            gt: 0
      actions:
        notify_slack:
          webhook:
            scheme: https
            host: hooks.slack.com
            port: 443
            method: post
            path: "/services/${SLACK_WEBHOOK_PATH}"
            body: >
              {"text": "⚠️ Elasticsearch snapshot failures detected!
              Failed: {{ctx.payload.stats.snapshots_failed}}
              Policy failures: {{ctx.payload.stats.policy_stats}}"}

    repository_health_alert:
      trigger:
        schedule:
          interval: 15m
      input:
        http:
          request:
            host: localhost
            port: 9200
            path: /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/_verify
            scheme: https
      condition:
        script:
          source: "return ctx.payload.nodes.size() == 0"
      actions:
        notify_pagerduty:
          webhook:
            scheme: https
            host: events.pagerduty.com
            port: 443
            method: post
            path: /v2/enqueue
            body: >
              {
                "routing_key": "${PAGERDUTY_ROUTING_KEY}",
                "event_action": "trigger",
                "payload": {
                  "summary": "Elasticsearch snapshot repository verification failed",
                  "severity": "critical",
                  "source": "elasticsearch-snapshot-monitor"
                }
              }

---
# File: runbooks/disaster-recovery-runbook.yml
# Disaster Recovery Runbook
disaster_recovery:
  description: "Step-by-step disaster recovery procedure using snapshots"

  prerequisites:
    - Identify the target snapshot for restoration
    - Ensure target cluster has sufficient disk space
    - Verify network connectivity to snapshot repository
    - Confirm no write operations to target indices

  steps:
    step_1:
      name: "Verify cluster health"
      action: "GET /_cluster/health"
      expected: "Cluster is accessible"

    step_2:
      name: "List available snapshots"
      action: "GET /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/_all?verbose=false"
      expected: "List of available snapshots with timestamps"

    step_3:
      name: "Get snapshot details"
      action: "GET /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{chosen_snapshot}"
      expected: "Snapshot state is SUCCESS"

    step_4:
      name: "Close indices to be restored (if they exist)"
      action: "POST /{index_pattern}/_close"
      expected: "Indices closed successfully"
      note: "Skip if indices don't exist on target"

    step_5:
      name: "Delete existing indices (if replacing)"
      action: "DELETE /{index_pattern}"
      expected: "Indices deleted"
      note: "Only if full replacement is intended"
      caution: "THIS IS DESTRUCTIVE - Verify before executing"

    step_6:
      name: "Start restoration"
      action: "POST /_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/{snapshot}/_restore"
      body:
        indices: "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
        index_settings:
          index.number_of_replicas: 0
      expected: "Restore initiated"

    step_7:
      name: "Monitor restoration progress"
      action: "GET /_cat/recovery?v&active_only=true"
      expected: "Recovery progress visible"
      poll_interval: 30s

    step_8:
      name: "Verify cluster health post-restore"
      action: "GET /_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=300s"
      expected: "Cluster health is yellow or green"

    step_9:
      name: "Increase replica count"
      action: "PUT /*/_settings"
      body:
        index:
          number_of_replicas: 1
      expected: "Replicas set to 1"

    step_10:
      name: "Wait for green cluster health"
      action: "GET /_cluster/health?wait_for_status=green&timeout=600s"
      expected: "Cluster health is green"

    step_11:
      name: "Validate document counts"
      action: "GET /_cat/indices?v&s=index"
      expected: "Document counts match expected values"

    step_12:
      name: "Run validation queries"
      action: "Custom validation queries against critical indices"
      expected: "Query results are correct"

  rollback:
    description: "If restoration fails"
    steps:
      - "Delete partially restored indices"
      - "Try restoration from previous snapshot"
      - "Contact engineering team if multiple snapshots fail"

---
# File: ansible/setup-snapshot-repos.yml
# Ansible playbook for automated snapshot setup
- name: Configure Elasticsearch Snapshot Repositories
  hosts: elasticsearch_master
  vars:
    es_host: "https://localhost:9200"
    es_user: "elastic"
    es_password: "{{ vault_es_password }}"
    s3_bucket: "elasticsearch-snapshots"
    s3_region: "us-east-1"
    s3_base_path: "backups/production"
    fs_backup_path: "/usr/share/elasticsearch/backup"

  tasks:
    - name: Add S3 repository plugin credentials to keystore
      elasticsearch_keystore:
        keys:
          - name: s3.client.default.access_key
            value: "{{ vault_aws_access_key }}"
          - name: s3.client.default.secret_key
            value: "{{ vault_aws_secret_key }}"

    - name: Register S3 snapshot repository
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_snapshot/s3-backup-repo"
        method: PUT
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
        body_format: json
        body:
          type: s3
          settings:
            bucket: "{{ s3_bucket }}"
            region: "{{ s3_region }}"
            base_path: "{{ s3_base_path }}"
            compress: true
            server_side_encryption: true
            storage_class: standard_ia
            max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
            max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb

    - name: Register filesystem snapshot repository
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_snapshot/fs-backup-repo"
        method: PUT
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
        body_format: json
        body:
          type: fs
          settings:
            location: "{{ fs_backup_path }}"
            compress: true
            max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
            max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb

    - name: Verify S3 repository
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_snapshot/s3-backup-repo/_verify"
        method: POST
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
      register: s3_verify_result

    - name: Verify filesystem repository
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_snapshot/fs-backup-repo/_verify"
        method: POST
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
      register: fs_verify_result

    - name: Create daily SLM policy
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_slm/policy/daily-full-snapshot"
        method: PUT
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
        body_format: json
        body:
          schedule: "0 0 2 * * ?"
          name: "<daily-snap-{now/d}>"
          repository: s3-backup-repo
          config:
            indices:
              - "*"
            ignore_unavailable: true
            include_global_state: true
            partial: false
          retention:
            expire_after: 30d
            min_count: 7
            max_count: 30

    - name: Create hourly SLM policy for critical indices
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_slm/policy/hourly-critical-snapshot"
        method: PUT
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
        body_format: json
        body:
          schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"
          name: "<hourly-critical-{now/H}>"
          repository: s3-backup-repo
          config:
            indices:
              - "critical-*"
              - "transactions-*"
              - "orders-*"
            ignore_unavailable: true
            include_global_state: false
            partial: false
          retention:
            expire_after: 2d
            min_count: 24
            max_count: 48

    - name: Create weekly SLM policy
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_slm/policy/weekly-archive-snapshot"
        method: PUT
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
        body_format: json
        body:
          schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"
          name: "<weekly-archive-{now/w}>"
          repository: s3-backup-repo
          config:
            indices:
              - "*"
            ignore_unavailable: true
            include_global_state: true
            partial: false
          retention:
            expire_after: 365d
            min_count: 4
            max_count: 52

    - name: Start SLM service
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_slm/start"
        method: POST
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"

    - name: Display SLM policy status
      uri:
        url: "{{ es_host }}/_slm/policy"
        method: GET
        user: "{{ es_user }}"
        password: "{{ es_password }}"
      register: slm_status

    - name: Print SLM status
      debug:
        var: slm_status.json
